name: Detroit Blanket-Lined Jacket
description: Ring-spun duck canvas with the original blanket lining intact and a corduroy collar that has softened in all the right places. Zip and snaps run clean, and the hem shows only light honest wear.

name: Type III Trucker Jacket
description: The boxy Type III cut in a mid stonewash that has faded evenly across the chest pleats. Rigid enough to hold its shape, broken in enough to wear straight away.

name: Denali Fleece Jacket
description: Dense Polartec fleece with almost no pilling and nylon overlays that still feel crisp. Full-length zip glides, and the elastic hem has plenty of life left.

name: Firebird Track Top
description: Glossy tricot with three crisp stripes down each sleeve and a slim, slightly cropped body. A faint sheen on the elbows is the only sign of age.

name: Quilted Liner Jacket
description: A nineties military-style liner, light as a shirt and warm as a sweater. One small repaired seam at the left cuff, otherwise sound throughout.

name: Swoosh Embroidered Crewneck
description: Heavyweight cotton fleece with a raised embroidered swoosh at the chest. Cuffs and collar still snap back, and the inside face is brushed and soft.

name: Reverse Weave Crewneck
description: The genuine reverse weave construction, with side gussets that keep the body from shrinking upward. Colour has settled into a warm, even maroon.

name: Midweight Logo Sweatshirt
description: Midweight fleece with the sleeve logo fully intact and ribbing that has kept its stretch. Roomy through the body, straight at the hem.

name: Trefoil Logo T-Shirt
description: Sun-faded cotton with a trefoil print that has cracked into a soft, worn-in texture. Light and thin in the best way.

name: Script Logo T-Shirt
description: Boxy cotton tee with the script logo screened across the chest. Collar sits flat and the body has kept its shape through many washes.

name: Single-Stitch Pocket Tee
description: Single-stitch hems and a chest pocket that sits exactly where it should. Thin, breathable cotton with a faint natural patina.

name: Classic Fit Oxford Shirt
description: Button-down oxford cloth with a soft roll to the collar and no fraying at the cuffs. Every button is original and secure.

name: Organic Cotton Work Shirt
description: A midweight organic cotton shirt cut long enough to wear open over a tee. Chest pockets sit square and the placket lies flat.

name: Brushed Flannel Overshirt
description: Heavy brushed cotton that has gone soft with age, cut generously enough to layer as a shirt jacket. Slight fading along the shoulders.

name: 874 Original Work Pant
description: The permanent-crease 874 with a straight leg and a clean hem. Twill still holds its structure with none of the shine that comes from overwear.

name: Duck Double-Knee Work Pant
description: Broken-in duck canvas with faded knee panels and a hammer loop still attached. Stiff at the waistband, soft everywhere it counts.

name: 501 Original Straight Jeans
description: Button fly, straight leg, and honest fading through the thigh and behind the knee. Hem is original with a light roping twist.

name: Selvedge Carpenter Jeans
description: Rigid selvedge denim with a utility pocket at the thigh and a clean red line at the outseam. Barely faded, ready to be worn in properly.

name: Synchilla Snap-T Pullover
description: Deep-pile Synchilla with a four-snap placket and a kangaroo pocket. Almost no matting on the body, which is rare for this style.

name: Cable-Knit Cotton Sweater
description: Chunky cotton cables with a crew neck that has held its shape. One small pull near the left cuff, invisible when worn.
